title: Remove useResourceSpecs workaround when backend exposes specDpp/specMachine/specMaterial via instanceVariables
description:
WORKAROUND: hooks/useResourceSpecs.ts queries all resourceSpecifications and finds DPP/Machine/Material by name because backend seeds these specs but doesn't expose them via instanceVariables.specs yet.

When backend adds specDpp, specMachine, specMaterial to instanceVariables response:
1. Remove QUERY_ALL_RESOURCE_SPECS fallback query
2. Simplify useResourceSpecs to only use instanceVariables
3. Remove findSpecByName fallback logic

Files affected:
- hooks/useResourceSpecs.ts (main workaround)
- lib/QueryAndMutation.ts (QUERY_ALL_RESOURCE_SPECS can be removed)
- components/SearchMachines.tsx (uses hook)
- components/SearchMaterials.tsx (uses hook)
- hooks/useProjectCRUD.ts (uses hook)
- hooks/useCitedResources.ts (uses hook)
- hooks/useProjectSpecs.ts (uses hook)

Refactor project creation: DPP and machines as economic resources
description:
Change how projects are saved: 1) DPP should be stored as a separate economic resource (not in metadata) and cited via economic event. 2) Machines should be stored as separate economic resources and cited. 3) Update CreateProjectForm UI to match Figma design (node-id=348-17380) with machines selection.

NOTE (2025-12): /products filtering is currently blocked by backend filter limitations. To unblock in GUI, we will ALSO persist derived tags on save:
- machine-<slug> for each selected/cited machine
- material-<slug> for each selected consumed material (consume event)
This enables filtering via existing classifiedAs/tag filter (see epic interfacer-gui-kjq and epic interfacer-gui-f61.13).

This epic remains focused on the ValueFlows economic resource model; the tag-based workaround is additive (not a replacement).

title: Document current vs new project creation architecture
description: Create comprehensive documentation showing: 1) Current flow: DPP stored as ULID in metadata JSON. 2) New flow: DPP as EconomicResource with cite event. 3) Machines as EconomicResource with cite events. 4) Data model changes needed. 5) Migration considerations. Document in history/ directory as ARCHITECTURE_REFACTOR.md

title: Test machines cited from projects
description: Verify machines integration: 1) Select machines during project creation. 2) Verify cite events created for each machine. 3) Query project to retrieve cited machines. 4) Test machine search/autocomplete. 5) Test removing selected machines. 6) Verify machines display on project detail page.

title: Update project detail page to show cited DPP and machines
description:
Modify project detail page (pages/project/[id].tsx) to display cited resources correctly after architecture refactor.

UPDATED REQUIREMENTS (superseded by task 14):
1. Query cited economic resources (DPP and machines) via economic events
   - Query events where action='cite' and inputOf=project's process
   - Filter by resourceInventoriedAs.conformsTo to separate DPP vs machines
   
2. Display DPP data from cited resource
   - Get DPP resource from cite events
   - Extract dppServiceUlid: JSON.parse(dppResource.metadata).dppServiceUlid
   - Fetch DPP document from interfacer-dpp using that ULID
   - Update ProjectDpp.tsx component if needed
   
3. Display cited machines with icons/names
   - Show machines section on project detail
   - Use machine names and map to icons from MACHINE_TYPES
   
4. Handle legacy projects gracefully
   - Check if project.metadata.dpp exists (old format)
   - Fall back to metadata approach for backwards compatibility
   
5. Add query for cited resources to lib/QueryAndMutation.ts

NOTE: Task 14 handles filtering in lists. This task focuses on detail page display.

title: Make linked design mandatory on creation
description:
Make selecting a linked Design mandatory during creation (at least for Product creation; ideally for any non-Design project type).

Acceptance:
- Create flow validation blocks submit without a linked design.
- Error shown inline on the design step.

title: Bind machine and material ResourceSpecs from backend instanceVariables
description:
Backend now provides specMachine and specMaterial in instanceVariables.specs query. Remove env variable fallbacks (NEXT_PUBLIC_SPEC_MACHINE, NEXT_PUBLIC_SPEC_MATERIAL) and use backend-provided IDs instead, matching how specProjectDesign/Product/Service work.

Files to update:
- lib/QueryAndMutation.ts: Add specMachine, specMaterial to QUERY_VARIABLES and QUERY_PROJECT_TYPES
- lib/types/index.ts: Update InstanceSpecs type
- hooks/useProjectCRUD.ts: Use specs.specMachine.id instead of RESOURCE_SPEC_MACHINE
- lib/resourceSpecs.ts: Deprecate/remove env-based constants

See history/PLAN_BACKEND_RESOURCE_SPECS.md for full plan.

title: Define DPP economic resource spec and GraphQL mutations
description: Define new ResourceSpecification for DPP resources. Create/update GraphQL mutations to: 1) Create DPP as EconomicResource (not just metadata). 2) Cite DPP resource from project via economic event. Need backend team coordination if schema changes required.

title: Define machines economic resource spec and data model
description: Define ONE ResourceSpecification for machines (not per machine type). Individual machines (Laser Cutter, 3D Printer, CNC, etc.) are EconomicResource instances conforming to this spec. Backend needs to create ResourceSpec 'Machine' or 'Equipment'. Machines differentiated by name field (e.g., 'Laser Cutter', '3D Printer'). Create mutations for citing machine resources from projects.

title: Query/create machine EconomicResource instances
description: Need to query existing machine EconomicResources or create them if they don't exist. Options: 1) Pre-seed backend with standard machines (Laser Cutter, 3D Printer, etc.) as global resources. 2) Create machines on-demand when first referenced. 3) Query interface to search/list available machines. Decide approach with backend team.

title: Add GraphQL mutation to cite machines from projects
description: Create mutation (or reuse CITE_PROJECT) to link project → machine resources. When user selects machines during project creation, create cite event for each machine. Mutation should accept array of machine resource IDs. Add to lib/QueryAndMutation.ts.

title: Add machines field to CreateProjectValues type
description: Extend CreateProjectValues interface to include machines: string[] (array of machine resource IDs). Update all form step types and validation schemas. Add to components/partials/create/project/CreateProjectForm.tsx type definitions.

title: Implement machine citation in handleProjectCreation
description: Extend handleProjectCreation in useProjectCRUD.ts to cite machines. After project created, loop through formData.machines and create cite event for each machine resource. Use CITE_PROJECT mutation with machine resource IDs. Handle errors gracefully if citation fails.

title: Create machines selection UI component
description: Implement machines selection interface from Figma (node-id=351-18123): 1) Toggle to enable/disable machines section. 2) Searchable dropdown to select machines (with icons). 3) Selected machines display as chips with remove button. 4) Machine search results with icons. Component should handle machine economic resource IDs.

title: Refactor DPP upload to create economic resource
description: Modify handleProjectCreation in useProjectCRUD.ts: 1) Change DPP flow to create EconomicResource instead of storing ULID in metadata. 2) Use cite economic event to link project -> DPP resource. 3) Update processDppValues to work with new model. 4) Keep interfacer-dpp service for DPP data storage but link via resource not metadata.

title: Add machines to project creation flow
description: Extend handleProjectCreation to handle machines: 1) Accept selected machine IDs from form. 2) Create cite events linking project to each machine resource. 3) Update CreateProjectValues type to include machines field. 4) Wire up machines selection UI to submission flow.

title: Update project metadata schema
description: Remove 'dpp' from metadata JSON schema in useProjectCRUD.ts line 237. Metadata should only contain: contributors, licenses, relations, declarations, remote, design. DPP and machines are now cited resources, not metadata. Update TypeScript types accordingly.

title: Update CreateProjectForm with Figma design sections
description: Update CreateProjectForm UI to match Figma design (node-id=348-17380): 1) Add 'Machines Needed' section with toggle. 2) Add 'Power Requirements' section (optional toggle). 3) Add 'Materials' section (optional toggle). 4) Add 'Manufacturing & Repair' section. 5) Add 'Environmental Impact' section. 6) Update form steps and validation schemas. Focus on machines first (P0), others can be P2/P3.

title: Test DPP as cited economic resource
description: Verify DPP creation flow: 1) Create project with DPP data. 2) Verify DPP created as EconomicResource. 3) Verify cite event links project to DPP. 4) Test DPP retrieval via traceDpp query. 5) Ensure existing projects still work. 6) Test error handling when DPP creation fails.

title: Fix /products page: filters, search, and results display
description:
Complete overhaul of /products page to make filters functional and results display correctly.

CURRENT STATE:
- ProductsFilters.tsx: UI renders but most filters do not filter results.
- useFilters hook: only applies q, tags, location (workaround), conformsTo, primaryAccountable.
- Backend EconomicResourceFilterParams: limited filter support (no metadata filtering).

WORKAROUND PLAN (client-side, no backend changes):
- Implement Machines Needed and Materials Needed filters by mapping selections to prefixed tags and using the existing classifiedAs (tags) filter.
- Tag format: machine-<slug>, material-<slug> (persisted at creation time by epic interfacer-gui-kjq).
- Implementation epic: interfacer-gui-f61.13.

FILTERS ACTUALLY APPLIED TO QUERY (today):
- conformsTo (show filter)
- classifiedAs (tags)
- orName (search query)
- orNote (location workaround)
- primaryAccountable

NOT APPLIED (backend doesn't support yet):
- manufacturability
- powerCompatibility / powerRequirement
- replicability
close_reason: Superseded by DTEC Epic 3 (Catalog Pages). Filters rebuilt in CatalogFilterSidebar with type-aware filter groups. Tag-based filter persistence still tracked in interfacer-gui-kjq.

title: Audit and fix working filters: search, tags, show (type)
description:
Verify and fix filters that should already work with current backend:

SEARCH (q parameter):
- ProductsSearchBar.tsx sets ?q=value
- useFilters reads q and sets orName in proposalFilter
- Verify FETCH_RESOURCES query actually receives and applies orName
- Test: search 'laser' should filter results

TAGS (tags parameter):
- ProductsFilters.tsx sets ?tags=tag1,tag2 via SelectTags
- useFilters reads tags and sets classifiedAs
- Verify classifiedAs filter works
- Test: select tag should filter results

SHOW/TYPE FILTER (show parameter):
- Sort dropdown has designs/products/services options
- useFilters.getShowFilterConformsTo() maps to conformsTo IDs
- Verify specProjectDesign, specProjectProduct, specProjectService IDs
- Test: selecting 'Designs' should show only design projects

LOCATION (location parameter):
- Uses orNote as workaround (not ideal)
- Verify workaround actually filters
- Document limitation for future spatial query support

FILES:
- hooks/useFilters.ts (lines 22-75)
- components/ProductsSearchBar.tsx
- components/ProductsFilters.tsx (tags section ~330-355)
- pages/products.tsx (show dropdown ~108-127)

title: Add URL state persistence for filters
description:
Filters should be shareable via URL and persist on refresh.

CURRENT:
- ProductsFilters updates URL params on Apply
- useFilters reads from router.query
- useEffect syncs URL to local state on mount

VERIFY:
1. Refreshing page preserves filters
2. Sharing URL with filters works
3. Back/forward navigation works
4. Deep linking to filtered view works

EXAMPLE URLs:
/products?q=laser&tags=electronics
/products?show=designs&location=Berlin

FILES:
- ProductsFilters.tsx - applyFilters() and useEffect for URL sync
- useFilters.ts - reads URL params

title: Add active filter chips/badges
description:
Show active filters as removable chips above results.

DESIGN:
- Chips below search bar, above results
- Each chip shows filter name and X to remove
- Clicking X removes that specific filter
- 'Clear all' link when multiple filters active

EXAMPLE:
[Tags: electronics ×] [Location: Berlin ×] [Type: Designs ×]  Clear all

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Create ActiveFilters component
2. Read active filters from URL/state
3. Render as removable chips
4. Handle individual filter removal
5. Handle clear all

FILES:
- Create components/ActiveFilters.tsx
- pages/products.tsx - add component
- Share filter state with ProductsFilters

FILES TO CHECK:

title: Workaround: Machines/Materials filters via prefixed tags
description:
Goal: make the existing Machines Needed and Materials Needed filters actually filter results, using backend-supported tag filtering.

Approach:
- Treat Machines/Materials filter selections as tag filters.
- Use tag strings machine-<slug> and material-<slug> and feed them into the existing classifiedAs filter.
- Keep UI options list aligned with the slugify rules used during save.

Dependency:
- Requires products to have machine-* / material-* tags persisted at save time (see epic interfacer-gui-kjq).

Acceptance:
- Selecting a machine/material filter updates URL state and results reflect the selection.
- No dependency on backend metadata filters.

title: Wire Machines Needed filter to machine-* tags
description:
Implement Machines Needed checkboxes to apply tag filtering.

Implementation notes:
- Convert selected machine options into tag strings machine-<slug>.
- Merge with existing tag filters (categories/tags) by writing into the same URL/state and classifiedAs filter.

Acceptance:
- Selecting Laser Cutter filters to products tagged machine-laser-cutter.

title: Wire Materials Needed filter to material-* tags
description:
Implement Materials Needed checkboxes to apply tag filtering.

Implementation notes:
- Convert selected material options into tag strings material-<slug>.
- Merge with existing tag filters and persist via URL state.

Acceptance:
- Selecting PLA filters to products tagged material-pla.

title: Align slugify rules between save-time tags and filter UI
description: Ensure the tag strings generated by the filter UI match exactly the tags persisted during creation.\n\nStatus: implemented via shared helper lib/tagging.ts used in both save-time tagging and /products filter mapping.

title: Products: manufacturability maps to spec filter
description:
On /products, implement Manufacturability radio as a ResourceSpecification filter (conformsTo) by setting show/spec:
- 'In progress' -> Designs
- 'Can be manufactured' -> Products

Acceptance:
- Selecting manufacturability changes results via conformsTo filter (no backend metadata needed).

title: Catalog: implement category/power/replicability/environment filters via prefixed classifiedAs tags
description:
Implement remaining /products sidebar filters from Figma (node 323:6277) using the same backend-compatible approach as machines/materials: derive deterministic classifiedAs tags with prefixes and filter via existing useFilters(tags->classifiedAs).

Scope (per spec):
- Fixed Categories
- Power compatibility
- Power requirement (range)
- Replicability
- Environmental impact (energy + CO2)

Non-scope: location (explicitly ignored)

Acceptance:
- Selecting any of these filters changes results (via tags) without backend changes.
- New tags are generated deterministically on create/edit, so filters match exactly.
- Active filters bar shows and allows removing these filters.
close_reason: Superseded: filter UI rebuilt in DTEC Epic 3 CatalogFilterSidebar. Backend tag persistence still in interfacer-gui-kjq.

title: Define tag taxonomy + helpers for new filter prefixes (category/power/replicability/environment)
description:
Define deterministic tag prefixes + slugging rules (reusing lib/tagging.ts) for:
- category-*
- powercompat-*
- replicability-*
- env-energy-* (range)
- env-co2-* (range)

For range filters, implement a bucket/threshold approach that works even if classifiedAs behaves like AND: generate monotonic tags like env-energy-ge-<threshold> and env-energy-le-<threshold> (same for power requirement + CO2).

Acceptance:
- One source of truth for tag generation for both save-time and filter-time.

title: Create flow: collect + persist category/power/replicability/environment fields as prefixed tags
description: Update the project/product creation form to collect: fixed categories, power compatibility, power requirement, replicability, environmental impact (energy + CO2). Persist as classifiedAs tags using the taxonomy defined in interfacer-gui-f61.15.1. Acceptance: new products include derived tags on create/edit; no free-form tags UI is reintroduced.

title: Products page UI: complete Figma sidebar filters (categories/power/replicability/environment)
description: Update ProductsFilters UI to match Figma node 323:6277 for: fixed categories, power compatibility, power requirement range, replicability, environmental impact (energy + CO2). On Apply, translate UI state to derived prefixed tags and merge into tags query param (like machines/materials). Acceptance: changing these filters changes results via useFilters(classifiedAs).

title: Active filters bar: show/remove new filter groups
description: Extend the active filters bar to include chips for categories, power compatibility, power requirement range, replicability, and environmental impact; removing a chip updates both the dedicated query params and removes the derived prefixed tags from tags=.

title: Tests: products filter interactions for new tag-based filters
description: Add tests verifying that applying/removing the new filters updates the URL and the GraphQL filter uses classifiedAs tags accordingly.

title: Implement machines filter (future backend-driven)
description:
Future enhancement (needs backend capabilities): populate Machines Needed filter from real machine EconomicResources and filter projects that cite those machine resources.

NOTE: For current unblock, implement tag-based filtering instead (see epic interfacer-gui-f61.13 and epic interfacer-gui-kjq). This task should only be resumed when backend provides a reliable way to filter by cited resources (or exposes machine resources list + filter support).

title: Fix results count display
description:
The results count is hardcoded and doesn't reflect actual filtered results. CURRENT CODE (pages/products.tsx line ~132):

{t("Showing 8 results")}

PROBLEM: - Always shows 'Showing 8 results' regardless of actual count - Doesn't update when filters are applied - Total count from FETCH_RESOURCES pageInfo.totalCount is available SOLUTION: 1. Get totalCount from ProjectsCards query response 2. Either: a) Pass count up via callback prop from ProjectsCards b) Move query to products.tsx and pass data down c) Use shared state/context for filter results 3. Update display to show: - 'Showing X results' (where X is actual count) - Or 'Showing X of Y results' if filtered RELATED CODE: - ProjectsCards.tsx uses FETCH_RESOURCES which returns pageInfo.totalCount - useLoadMore hook processes pagination data - ProductsHeader.tsx shows totalProjects stat (also needs fixing) ACCEPTANCE: - Results count matches actual displayed items - Count updates when filters change - Loading state while count is being fetched

title: Fix ProductsHeader stats (total projects, available, manufacturers)
description:
ProductsHeader shows 3 stat cards with hardcoded/fake values.

CURRENT CODE (pages/products.tsx lines ~55-60):
const totalProjects = data?.economicResources?.pageInfo?.totalCount || 18429;
const projectsAvailable = Math.floor(totalProjects * 0.15) || 2847; // Approximate calculation
const manufacturers = 512; // This would need a separate query

PROBLEMS:
1. totalProjects: Falls back to hardcoded 18429
2. projectsAvailable: Arbitrary 15% calculation, meaningless
3. manufacturers: Completely hardcoded 512

WHAT STATS SHOULD MEAN:
1. Total Projects - All projects in system (current query is correct)
2. Projects Available - What does 'available' mean? Clarify with design:
   - Projects with files?
   - Projects that can be manufactured?
   - Projects with complete documentation?
3. Manufacturers - Count of unique primaryAccountable users
   - Requires separate query: count distinct primaryAccountable.id

SOLUTIONS:
1. totalProjects: Already works via GET_PRODUCTS_STATS query
2. projectsAvailable: Either remove or define clear meaning
3. manufacturers: Add GraphQL query for unique users count

NEW QUERY NEEDED:
query GetManufacturersCount {
  # Need backend support for distinct count
  # Or fetch all and count client-side (not ideal for large datasets)
}

ALTERNATIVE: Remove stats that can't be accurately calculated

title: Remove tags display from overview tab
Workaround: Persist machine/material tags on product save
description:
Goal: unblock /products filtering without waiting for backend metadata filters by persisting machine/material info as standard tags.

Approach (GUI-side):
- When saving a Project Product that cites one or more machines, also add tags for each machine using a stable prefix format: machine-<slug> (e.g., machine-laser-cutter).
- Do the same for materials: user selects materials consumed; create consume economic events (1 per material) and also add tags material-<slug>.
- Remove manual/free-form tag insertion from the creation form (tags become derived/controlled; category/tag selection for browsing remains on /products).

Acceptance:
- Newly created/edited products persist machine-* and material-* tags consistently.
- Tag format is deterministic (slugify rules documented) so filter UI can generate exact tag strings.
- No backend changes required except pre-creating material economic resources conforming to a Material ResourceSpecification (tracked separately).
close_reason: Duplicate of interfacer-gui-kjq (machine/material tag persistence workaround).

title: Workaround: Persist machine/material tags on product save
description:
Goal: unblock /products filtering without waiting for backend metadata filters by persisting machine/material info as standard tags.

Approach (GUI-side):
- When saving a Project Product that cites one or more machines, also add tags for each machine using prefix machine-<slug> (e.g., machine-laser-cutter).
- Do the same for materials: user selects materials consumed; create consume economic events (1 per material) and also add tags material-<slug>.
- Remove manual/free-form tag insertion from the creation form.

Acceptance:
- Newly created/edited products persist machine-* and material-* tags consistently.
- Tag format is deterministic (slugify rules documented) so filter UI can generate exact tag strings.
- Backend prerequisite: material EconomicResources exist for selection (tracked as separate dependency).

title: Add machine-* tags on product save
description:
When creating/updating a Project Product, derive tags from selected/cited machines and persist them as classifiedAs tags.

Rules:
- For each selected machine name, add tag machine-<slug> (lowercase, spaces->-, strip non-alphanum/-).
- Avoid duplicates; keep existing unrelated tags intact.

Acceptance:
- Creating a product with Laser Cutter + 3D Printer results in tags [machine-laser-cutter, machine-3d-printer] present on the saved resource.

title: Add materials selection + consume events + material-* tags
description:
Implement materials consumed flow analogous to machines, with backend-precreated material EconomicResources.

UI:
- Add materials picker in CreateProjectForm (or equivalent) to select one or more materials to be consumed.

Data:
- For each selected material, create an EconomicEvent with action=consume linking the project/process to the material resource.
- Also persist tags material-<slug> on the product resource.

Acceptance:
- Selecting PLA and ABS creates 2 consume events and adds tags [material-pla, material-abs].
- If backend provides no materials list, UI shows an empty/disabled state (no crash).

title: (Later) Remove tag/category selectors from creation form
description:
Deferred cleanup: remove every tag/category selector from the project/product creation flow.

Rationale:
- Tags will be derived automatically only for machine/material requirements (machine-* and material-*).
- Categories/tags are not user-entered during creation in this UX.

Acceptance:
- No tag/category selector UI remains in the creation form.
- Save still auto-adds derived machine-* and material-* tags.

title: Backend prerequisite: pre-create material resources
description:
Dependency: backend must expose a Material ResourceSpecification and pre-created material EconomicResources for selection.

Acceptance:
- GraphQL query can list available materials (EconomicResource) for selection.
- Each material has a stable name suitable for slugging.

Use Figma design tokens: Head/H1 font, Primary color #036A53, Surface #FFFFFF. Stats should be fetched from GraphQL or calculated from data.

Each filter should update URL query params and trigger data refetch.

title: Create product card grid component
description: Build ProductCardGrid component displaying products in responsive grid layout. Each card shows: product image, title, description, badges (Can be Manufactured, etc), machine icons, license info, and like count. Use Shadow/sm for card elevation. Support hover states.

title: Implement search bar with filters integration
description: Add search functionality at the top of the main content area. Should search across project names, descriptions, and tags. Integrate with existing SearchBar component but customize for products filtering. Update URL query params and trigger filtered results.

title: Add Sort by and Show controls
description: Implement sorting dropdown (Latest, Oldest, Most Liked, etc) and 'Show All' selector. Position in top-right of content area. Sorting should update query params and re-fetch data accordingly.

title: Implement GraphQL query for products filtering
description: Create or extend GraphQL query to support all new filter parameters: manufacturability, machines, materials, location, tags, power specs, replicability. Should return paginated results with total counts for stats display.

title: Add pagination controls and infinite scroll
description: Implement pagination at bottom of product grid. Show results count (e.g., 'Showing 8 results'). Consider infinite scroll or Load More button. Should work with GraphQL cursor-based pagination.

title: Make layout responsive for mobile and tablet
description: Ensure the products page works on all screen sizes. Sidebar filters should collapse to drawer/modal on mobile. Product grid should adapt from 3 columns (desktop) to 2 (tablet) to 1 (mobile). Test on various breakpoints.

title: Fix project cards: use real data for stars, licenses, and requirements
description:
Fix ProjectCard/GeneralCard components to use real project data instead of hardcoded/mock values.

CURRENT ISSUES:
1. Star count is hardcoded ('3.9k') in GeneralCard.StarCount component
2. License display uses fallback 'CERN-OHL-W' instead of real project license data
3. Resource requirements shows mock text instead of actual project requirements
4. Star functionality exists (AddStar component with useSocial hook) but count display is fake

COMPONENTS AFFECTED:
- components/GeneralCard.tsx - Main card component with subcomponents
  - StarCount() - Line ~257, hardcoded '3.9k'
  - LicenseFooter() - Line ~109, uses project.metadata?.license || 'CERN-OHL-W'
  - ResourceRequirements() - Line ~86, uses project.metadata?.requirements || mock text
  - UserAndStarHeader() - Uses AddStar component correctly
- components/ProjectCard.tsx - Wrapper using GeneralCard
- components/AddStar.tsx - Star button using useSocial hook (working correctly)

EXISTING HOOKS:
- hooks/useSocial.ts - Provides likeER, isLiked, erFollowerLength functionality
  - Can get like count for economic resources
  - Already used in AddStar component for like/unlike functionality
  - Need to expose star/like count data

PROJECT DATA STRUCTURE:
- EconomicResource type in lib/types
- project.metadata - Contains various metadata
- project.conformsTo - Project type info
- Social data available through useSocial hook

SOLUTION:
1. Use useSocial hook in GeneralCard to get real star count
2. Extract real license from project data (check project.metadata, project.license, etc.)
3. Extract real requirements from project metadata or related resources
4. Ensure data flows correctly through GeneralCard context
close_reason: Superseded by DTEC redesign. ProjectCardNew uses real data via useSocial hook, real licenses, and type-aware footers.

title: Integrate useSocial hook for real star counts in GeneralCard

Make StarCount component clickable to allow users to star/unstar projects.

COMPLETED:

REMAINING:
- Make StarCount clickable (button instead of div)
- Use likeER function from useSocial to star/unstar
- Show filled/outline star icon based on isLiked state
- Add hover effects
- Handle authenticated/non-authenticated users

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Transform StarCount from display-only to interactive button

- Similar to AddStar component but integrated in card overlay
